target.title: fix(codex): guard app-server context budget
target.body:
## Summary

- Rotate a bound Codex app-server native thread before resume when the native rollout crosses the 70k active token guard or the configured active transcript byte limit.
- Cap large native tool results before they are returned into the Codex app-server thread, with a truncation notice that asks for narrower args.
- Prefer `last_token_usage` from Codex token-count events and use native rollout tokens over mirrored OpenClaw session totals when deciding whether to rotate, so a stale post-compaction session total does not force repeated false rotations.

## Why

A local WebChat setup could become slow after a large Codex app-server thread was resumed repeatedly. Manual compaction left the mirrored OpenClaw session total high while the active native rollout could be small again. The previous guard shape used the max of mirrored session tokens and native rollout tokens, which could treat a fresh native thread as over-budget forever after compaction.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** WebChat/Codex app-server resumes could keep carrying or repeatedly rotating oversized native transcripts. The stale-token edge is that `sessions.json` can still show an over-budget mirrored OpenClaw total after compaction while the active native rollout has a much smaller `last_token_usage`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw setup, OpenClaw `2026.5.16-beta.3`, Gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`, default `main` agent, Codex app-server runtime, OpenAI OAuth model path using `openai/gpt-5.5`, QMD memory backend.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Applied the equivalent local hot patch to the installed OpenClaw runtime and preservation script, restarted Gateway, then ran `node ~/.openclaw/workspace/systems/reapply-openclaw-latency-patches.js --dry-run`, `openclaw health --json`, `openclaw memory status --json`, and `node ~/.openclaw/workspace/systems/openclaw-context-budget-check.js --json`. Also ran the focused upstream regression lane with `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=<tmp-json> OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts --testNamePattern 'native rollout|stale mirrored'`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the patched real OpenClaw install:

```text
$ node ~/.openclaw/workspace/systems/reapply-openclaw-latency-patches.js --dry-run
- codex-native-thread-size-rotation: already patched or pattern not present (run-attempt-DEhr_oag.js)
- codex-native-tool-result-cap: already patched or pattern not present (thread-lifecycle-CPF2Zs5N.js)
Done. Changed files: 0.

$ openclaw health --json | jq '{ok,eventLoop:.eventLoop,telegram:.channels.telegram.connected}'
{
  "ok": true,
  "eventLoop": {
    "degraded": false,
    "reasons": [],
    "intervalMs": 9306,
    "delayP99Ms": 22.3,
    "delayMaxMs": 23.4,
    "utilization": 0.02,
    "cpuCoreRatio": 0.019
  },
  "telegram": true
}

$ node ~/.openclaw/workspace/systems/openclaw-context-budget-check.js --json | jq '{sessionTokens:.session.totalTokens,nativeTokens:.nativeRollout.maxTokens,rotateBeforeResume:.flags.rotateBeforeResume,overNative:.flags.overNativeTokenGuard,overContext:.flags.overConfiguredContext}'
{
  "sessionTokens": 96938,
  "nativeTokens": 97327,
  "rotateBeforeResume": true,
  "overNative": true,
  "overContext": true
}

$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=<tmp-json> OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts --testNamePattern 'native rollout|stale mirrored'
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed | 143 skipped (146)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The patched local runtime remains healthy and reports the currently over-budget native rollout as `rotateBeforeResume: true`, which is the intended next-resume behavior. The new regression test covers the post-compaction stale-token case: mirrored session total `96000`, native `total_token_usage` `97000`, native `last_token_usage` `12000`; the app-server resumes the bound thread instead of starting a new one.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run the full repository test suite locally. I did not post a new WebChat prompt after opening this PR; the local runtime was validated by Gateway health, QMD memory status, patch reapply dry-run, and the focused regression lane.

## Validation

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=<tmp-json> OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts --testNamePattern 'native rollout|stale mirrored'`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
target.author: hansolo949
